request.objectPrompt:
Shared natural Uniswap biome scatter props for grass, dirt, sand, forest floor, stone, swamp, and lake edges.
A cost-efficient batch sheet of separate static Uniswap biome and land scatter props.
Each cell is a different independent object, not a variant or animation frame.
These are natural biome objects for terrain richness: stones, bushes, reeds, moss, flowers, crystals, puddles, roots, fallen branches, and magical habitat details.
Avoid buildings, furniture, civic architecture, tools, signs, benches, tables, and town decorations.
Use the row and column map exactly.
Row 0 column 0: Violet Clover Bush - dense violet clover bush with mint leaf tips, pearl dew, and a rounded playable silhouette.
Row 0 column 1: Mint Moss Stone - small lavender-gray field stone partly covered in mint moss and violet clover.
Row 0 column 2: Pearl Sand Shells - tiny pearl shell and lavender pebble cluster for lake sand edges.
Row 0 column 3: Oracle Glass Crystal - transparent cyan oracle glass crystal growing from damp moss.
Row 1 column 0: Liquidity Reeds - thin teal reeds with pink seed tips for water and swamp edges.
Row 1 column 1: Route Silk Grass - tuft of pearly route-silk grass braided through mint leaves.
Row 1 column 2: Fee Moss Mushrooms - small stepped mint, teal, and violet mushroom cluster.
Row 1 column 3: Unicorn Flower Clump - violet and unicorn-pink meadow flowers with mint leaves.
Row 2 column 0: Swapstone Pebbles - rose-quartz swapstone pebbles with powdered lavender dust.
Row 2 column 1: Fallen Unicorn Branch - fallen pale mint branch with pink fronds and pearl seed pods.
Row 2 column 2: Liquidity Puddle - tiny shallow teal puddle with pink and violet paired currents.
Row 2 column 3: Governance Stone Outcrop - natural lavender-gray stone outcrop with flat worn tops and mint moss seams.
Row 3 column 0: Forest Root Cluster - exposed mint roots curling through dark violet forest floor.
Row 3 column 1: Pink Thistle - small unicorn-pink thistle with soft spiky violet leaves.
Row 3 column 2: Swamp Bubble Moss - wet mint moss pad with tiny cyan bubbles and pink wetland dots.
Row 3 column 3: Violet Log - short fallen violet-brown log with mint moss and tiny pink fungi.
request.stylePrompt: cozy hand-painted 2D biome scatter props, three-quarter top-down view, transparent background, crisp readable silhouettes at small scale, Uniswap-inspired violet, mint, pearl, pink, cyan, and lavender-gray palette, natural stones, bushes, reeds, moss, crystals, flowers, roots, puddles, and fallen branches, soft natural edges, warm but balanced highlights, no readable text, no logos, no UI

image.title: Uniswap Nature Props sprite sheet
image.prompt:
Create a game object sprite sheet for Uniswap Nature Props.
Object brief: Shared natural Uniswap biome scatter props for grass, dirt, sand, forest floor, stone, swamp, and lake edges.
A cost-efficient batch sheet of separate static Uniswap biome and land scatter props.
Each cell is a different independent object, not a variant or animation frame.
These are natural biome objects for terrain richness: stones, bushes, reeds, moss, flowers, crystals, puddles, roots, fallen branches, and magical habitat details.
Avoid buildings, furniture, civic architecture, tools, signs, benches, tables, and town decorations.
Use the row and column map exactly.
Row 0 column 0: Violet Clover Bush - dense violet clover bush with mint leaf tips, pearl dew, and a rounded playable silhouette.
Row 0 column 1: Mint Moss Stone - small lavender-gray field stone partly covered in mint moss and violet clover.
Row 0 column 2: Pearl Sand Shells - tiny pearl shell and lavender pebble cluster for lake sand edges.
Row 0 column 3: Oracle Glass Crystal - transparent cyan oracle glass crystal growing from damp moss.
Row 1 column 0: Liquidity Reeds - thin teal reeds with pink seed tips for water and swamp edges.
Row 1 column 1: Route Silk Grass - tuft of pearly route-silk grass braided through mint leaves.
Row 1 column 2: Fee Moss Mushrooms - small stepped mint, teal, and violet mushroom cluster.
Row 1 column 3: Unicorn Flower Clump - violet and unicorn-pink meadow flowers with mint leaves.
Row 2 column 0: Swapstone Pebbles - rose-quartz swapstone pebbles with powdered lavender dust.
Row 2 column 1: Fallen Unicorn Branch - fallen pale mint branch with pink fronds and pearl seed pods.
Row 2 column 2: Liquidity Puddle - tiny shallow teal puddle with pink and violet paired currents.
Row 2 column 3: Governance Stone Outcrop - natural lavender-gray stone outcrop with flat worn tops and mint moss seams.
Row 3 column 0: Forest Root Cluster - exposed mint roots curling through dark violet forest floor.
Row 3 column 1: Pink Thistle - small unicorn-pink thistle with soft spiky violet leaves.
Row 3 column 2: Swamp Bubble Moss - wet mint moss pad with tiny cyan bubbles and pink wetland dots.
Row 3 column 3: Violet Log - short fallen violet-brown log with mint moss and tiny pink fungi..

Reference image 1 is a checkerboard layout guide. Use it as the exact composition template with exact sizes, all cells are square all equal size.
Preserve its 4:4 sheet ratio and its 4 columns by 4 rows of equal square cells.
The exact guide has also been saved locally at generated/object-sprites/uniswap-nature-props/object-sprite-layout-guide.png for verification.
Place exactly one frame in the center of each checkerboard cell.
Align the center of each object frame to the center of its cell.
Keep every frame fully inside its own cell, with consistent margins and no overlap into neighboring cells.
Keep the checkerboard grid visible in the final image so the generated frames can be verified against the grid.
Preserve the alternating white and gray cell backgrounds from the guide.
Draw the object frames on top of the provided checkerboard cells.

Target runtime atlas metadata: 1024x1024 logical units, arranged as 4 rows by 4 columns.
Each logical grid cell is 256x256 units. The generated image may be higher resolution, but it must keep the same 4:4 grid ratio.
Keep the object scale, anchor point, perspective, and lighting consistent across cells.
Do not crop any object. Leave enough breathing room inside each cell for animation motion.
Do not draw labels, text, numbers, watermarks, decorative borders, or UI.
Use a transparent background for every cell.

Rows, top to bottom:
Row 0: Prop Row 0 (row-0) - column 0: Violet Clover Bush - dense violet clover bush with mint leaf tips, pearl dew, and a rounded playable silhouette; column 1: Mint Moss Stone - small lavender-gray field stone partly covered in mint moss and violet clover; column 2: Pearl Sand Shells - tiny pearl shell and lavender pebble cluster for lake sand edges; column 3: Oracle Glass Crystal - transparent cyan oracle glass crystal growing from damp moss
Row 1: Prop Row 1 (row-1) - column 0: Liquidity Reeds - thin teal reeds with pink seed tips for water and swamp edges; column 1: Route Silk Grass - tuft of pearly route-silk grass braided through mint leaves; column 2: Fee Moss Mushrooms - small stepped mint, teal, and violet mushroom cluster; column 3: Unicorn Flower Clump - violet and unicorn-pink meadow flowers with mint leaves
Row 2: Prop Row 2 (row-2) - column 0: Swapstone Pebbles - rose-quartz swapstone pebbles with powdered lavender dust; column 1: Fallen Unicorn Branch - fallen pale mint branch with pink fronds and pearl seed pods; column 2: Liquidity Puddle - tiny shallow teal puddle with pink and violet paired currents; column 3: Governance Stone Outcrop - natural lavender-gray stone outcrop with flat worn tops and mint moss seams
Row 3: Prop Row 3 (row-3) - column 0: Forest Root Cluster - exposed mint roots curling through dark violet forest floor; column 1: Pink Thistle - small unicorn-pink thistle with soft spiky violet leaves; column 2: Swamp Bubble Moss - wet mint moss pad with tiny cyan bubbles and pink wetland dots; column 3: Violet Log - short fallen violet-brown log with mint moss and tiny pink fungi

Columns, left to right:
Column 0: slot 1.
Column 1: slot 2.
Column 2: slot 3.
Column 3: slot 4.

Column behavior:
This is a batch sheet of separate static world props, not animation frames.
Each cell must contain the specific prop named by its row and column.
Do not evolve, transform, or animate objects across columns.
Keep perspective, lighting, palette, shadow softness, and scale language consistent across all cells.
Small props may leave more transparent space inside their cell; large landmarks should still fit fully inside their cell.
Every prop must be isolated on a transparent background and usable as an independent in-world object.

Style direction:
cozy hand-painted 2D biome scatter props, three-quarter top-down view, transparent background, crisp readable silhouettes at small scale, Uniswap-inspired violet, mint, pearl, pink, cyan, and lavender-gray palette, natural stones, bushes, reeds, moss, crystals, flowers, roots, puddles, and fallen branches, soft natural edges, warm but balanced highlights, no readable text, no logos, no UI
Reference image 2 is a style reference. Use it for palette, rendering texture, edge softness, contrast, and game-art finish. Do not copy its subject unless it matches the object brief.

Return one PNG containing the full sprite sheet only.
